get.description: Force a call to all healthchecks registered.
Retrieve operational information about this instance of TesseractModule.
Return the public schema with all the available cubes.
Return the public schema for the single specified cube.
Redirect the request to the canonical endpoint in jsonrecords format.
Execute a request for data from the server.

parameters.name: cube

schema.description: The name of the cube to work with.
schema.title: Cube

parameters.description: The name of the cube to work with.

parameters.name: drilldowns

schema.description: A list of the level names to slice the bulk of the aggregated data.
schema.title: Drilldowns

parameters.description: A list of the level names to slice the bulk of the aggregated data.

parameters.name: measures

schema.description: A list of the measure names to retrieve from the aggregated data.
schema.title: Measures

parameters.description: A list of the measure names to retrieve from the aggregated data.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description:
Specifies pagination on the results, so the query can be separated in multiple requests.

The shape of the parameter is composed by one integer, or two integers separated by a comma:
    `{value}` := `{limit}` | `{limit},{offset}`
Where:
    `{limit}` : `int`, defines the max amount of items in the response data
    `{offset}` : `int`, defines the index of the first item in the full list where the list in the response data will start.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description:
Specifies pagination on the results, so the query can be separated in multiple requests.

The shape of the parameter is composed by one integer, or two integers separated by a comma:
    `{value}` := `{limit}` | `{limit},{offset}`
Where:
    `{limit}` : `int`, defines the max amount of items in the response data
    `{offset}` : `int`, defines the index of the first item in the full list where the list in the response data will start.

parameters.name: parents

schema.description:
Specifies if the request should include the labels for the parent levels of the selected drilldowns.

The shape for the value is:
    `{value}` := `{truthy}` | `{falsey}` | `{name},{name...}`
Where:
    `{truthy}` : A string that can be interpreted as a truthy value; retrieves parents for all drilldowns in the request;
    `{falsey}` : A string that can be interpreted as a falsey value; deactivates parents for all drilldowns in the request (default);
    `{name}` : `str`, the name of the specific drilldown(s) to get parents for.
schema.title: Parents

parameters.description:
Specifies if the request should include the labels for the parent levels of the selected drilldowns.

The shape for the value is:
    `{value}` := `{truthy}` | `{falsey}` | `{name},{name...}`
Where:
    `{truthy}` : A string that can be interpreted as a truthy value; retrieves parents for all drilldowns in the request;
    `{falsey}` : A string that can be interpreted as a falsey value; deactivates parents for all drilldowns in the request (default);
    `{name}` : `str`, the name of the specific drilldown(s) to get parents for.

parameters.name: ranking

schema.description:
Adds a Ranking column to the specified measures.

If unset, or set to a value that can be interpreted as falsey, no rankings are added.
If set to a value that can be interpreted as truthy, a ranking for every measure is added.
Any other string is interpreted as a comma-separated list of the measure names that are expected to be added a ranking.

The value is always an unsigned integer. If many rows have an equal value, all are assigned the same ranking value, and the next value skips the places for the repeated rows.
The direction of the ranking can be specified by (not) prefixing the measure name with a minus character (`-`): the ranking will be calculated in descending order if the minus is present, ascending otherwise.
schema.title: Ranking

parameters.description:
Adds a Ranking column to the specified measures.

If unset, or set to a value that can be interpreted as falsey, no rankings are added.
If set to a value that can be interpreted as truthy, a ranking for every measure is added.
Any other string is interpreted as a comma-separated list of the measure names that are expected to be added a ranking.

The value is always an unsigned integer. If many rows have an equal value, all are assigned the same ranking value, and the next value skips the places for the repeated rows.
The direction of the ranking can be specified by (not) prefixing the measure name with a minus character (`-`): the ranking will be calculated in descending order if the minus is present, ascending otherwise.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description:
Specifies the order of the results; available for properties and measures included in the request.

The shape for the value is:
    `{value}` := `{field}` | `{field}.{order}`
Where:
    `{field}` : `str`, defines the field to use: Measure or Property
    `{order}` : `Literal["asc", "desc"]`, defines the direction to sort the dataset

The field will be resolved to a Measure first, if not found then to a Property.
When `{order}` is not set, `"asc"` will be used by default.
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description:
Specifies the order of the results; available for properties and measures included in the request.

The shape for the value is:
    `{value}` := `{field}` | `{field}.{order}`
Where:
    `{field}` : `str`, defines the field to use: Measure or Property
    `{order}` : `Literal["asc", "desc"]`, defines the direction to sort the dataset

The field will be resolved to a Measure first, if not found then to a Property.
When `{order}` is not set, `"asc"` will be used by default.

parameters.name: exclude

schema.description:
Specifies which values should be omitted from the aggregation.

The value is composed by multiple cut definitions separated by semicolons:
    `{value}` := `{cut_def};{cut_def...}`
Where a single cut definition is composed by the Level name, colon, and the list of keys separated by commas:
    `{cut_def}` := `{name}:{key},{key...}`
    `{name}` : `str`, the name of the Level to apply the cut
    `{key}` : `str | int`, the members' ID values
schema.title: Exclude

parameters.description:
Specifies which values should be omitted from the aggregation.

The value is composed by multiple cut definitions separated by semicolons:
    `{value}` := `{cut_def};{cut_def...}`
Where a single cut definition is composed by the Level name, colon, and the list of keys separated by commas:
    `{cut_def}` := `{name}:{key},{key...}`
    `{name}` : `str`, the name of the Level to apply the cut
    `{key}` : `str | int`, the members' ID values

parameters.name: include

schema.description:
Specifies which values should be considered for the aggregation.

The value is composed by multiple cut definitions separated by semicolons:
    `{value}` := `{cut_def};{cut_def...}`
Where a single cut definition is composed by the Level name, colon, and the list of keys separated by commas:
    `{cut_def}` := `{name}:{key},{key...}`
    `{name}` : `str`, the name of the Level to apply the cut
    `{key}` : `str | int`, the members' ID values

For compatibility, this parameter also accepts the definition of the {name} parameter directly in the URL search params; note the level name must start in caps for this to work. This means both `&include=Year:2009,2010` and `&Year=2009,2010` are equivalent.
schema.title: Include

parameters.description:
Specifies which values should be considered for the aggregation.

The value is composed by multiple cut definitions separated by semicolons:
    `{value}` := `{cut_def};{cut_def...}`
Where a single cut definition is composed by the Level name, colon, and the list of keys separated by commas:
    `{cut_def}` := `{name}:{key},{key...}`
    `{name}` : `str`, the name of the Level to apply the cut
    `{key}` : `str | int`, the members' ID values

For compatibility, this parameter also accepts the definition of the {name} parameter directly in the URL search params; note the level name must start in caps for this to work. This means both `&include=Year:2009,2010` and `&Year=2009,2010` are equivalent.

schema.title: Filters

cube.title: Cube
cube.description: The cube to retrieve the main data

drilldowns.title: Drilldowns
drilldowns.description: A list of the level names to slice the bulk of the aggregated data.

include.title: Members to include
include.description: Limits the results returned by the output. Only members of the declared Level will be considered in the data.

exclude.title: Members to exclude
exclude.description: Limits the results returned by the output. Members of the declared Level won't be considered in the data.

locale.title: Locale code
locale.description: Defines the locale variation for the labels in the data

parents.title: Include parent levels
parents.description: Specifies if the response items should include the parent levels for activity and location.

time.title: Filter by time
time.description: Specifies the time period to filter the data by

ACSParameters.title: ACSParameters
ACSParameters.description: Parameters needed to perform a PUMS calculation.

CutIntent.title: CutIntent
CutIntent.description:
Filtering instructions for a qualitative value.

Instances of this class are used to define cut parameters.
Its values are directly inputted by the user, so should never be considered
valid by itself.

DataRequest.title: DataRequest
DataRequest.description:
Represents the intent for a Data Query made by the user.

All its properties are defined by strings of the names of the components
from the schema.
None of these parameters are verified during construction, so it's possible
for the query to be invalid; a subclass of :class:`backend.exceptions.BackendError`
will be raised in that case.
The only purpose of this structure is containing and passing over the query
intent to the internals.

During a request, a :class:`Query` instance is constructed with objects from
a schema, using parameters from this instance.

DataType.title: DataType
DataType.description: Types of the data the user can expect to find in the associated column.

DimensionType.title: DimensionType
DimensionType.description: Kind of data a dimension is storing.

FilterIntent.title: FilterIntent
FilterIntent.description:
Filtering instructions for a quantitative value.

Instances of this class are used to define filter parameters.
Its values are directly inputted by the user, so should never be considered
valid by itself.

GrowthIntent.title: GrowthIntent
GrowthIntent.description: Calculation of growth with respect to a time parameter and a measure.

JoinIntent.title: JoinIntent
JoinIntent.description: Specifies the intent of the user to perform a Join operation between 2 datasets.

JoinType.title: JoinType
JoinType.description: Defines the different types of join operations available to the user.

LogicOperator.title: LogicOperator
LogicOperator.description: Defines logical operations between conditional predicates.

Membership.title: Membership
Membership.description: Specifies the membership relation of a value to a set.

TimeRestriction.title: TimeRestriction
TimeRestriction.description:
Time-axis filtering instructions for internal use.

Instances of this class are used to define a time restriction over the
resulting data. It must always contain both fields.

TimeScale.title: TimeScale
TimeScale.description: Specifies a scale of detail in time.

TopkIntent.title: TopkIntent
TopkIntent.description:
Limits the results to the K first/last elements in subsets determined by one or more levels and their associated value.

Adds a column that indicates the position of each element in that ranking.
